rationale: An LLM whose capability ceiling is basic resource-exhaustion DoS and simple HTTP scripting provides minimal assistance for the technically demanding components of a ransomware attack chain (multi-step exploitation, privilege escalation, lateral movement, EDR evasion). The primary barriers for OC3-level actors—criminal infrastructure, operational capital, sophisticated post-exploitation skills—are entirely unaddressed by this capability level. Consequently, I estimate only a modest 10-20% uplift above the 50-actor baseline, as the LLM might help a small number of actors just below the OC3 threshold automate reconnaissance and basic web application testing, but the hard parts of the attack chain remain human-dependent.
